seo_title: YouTube starts automatically labeling AI-generated content on its…
meta_description: YouTube is now taking AI labeling seriously by relocating disclosures and automatically identifying AI-generated content.
body_html:
YouTube will now automatically label AI-generated content on its platform, making it easier for viewers to spot.

AI disclosures will be relocated to the main stage of videos, making them more visible to viewers. The new label format will apply to all photorealistic and meaningfully AI altered or generated content on YouTube. YouTube is rolling out new internal signals to automatically identify and label AI-generated videos.

AI Label The new label will appear directly below the video player, above the description.

Shorts Overlay The AI label will also appear as an overlay on YouTube Shorts.

Viewers get the context they need at a glance, with the new label format making it easier to spot AI-generated content. Creators must still manually disclose when using photorealistic AI, but the new system will help identify and label more AI content.

Next Steps YouTube says it's committed to establishing a system that the platform actually sticks with, after inconsistent labeling practices in the past.

source.title: YouTube is putting AI labels where you’ll actually see them
source.summary: YouTube is making changes to its AI labeling policy, moving AI disclosures from less visible locations to more prominent spots on Shorts and long-form videos. The platform will also begin automatically identifying and labeling AI-generated content. This move aims to increase transparency about AI use on the site.
